model_narrative: This is a daily power procurement and unit commitment problem for an electric utility. Over a sequence of quarter-hour time slices we must serve a forecast power demand in every slice. Power can come from three sources: our own power plant, which we run by choosing an operating stage in each slice, spot market contracts that we buy as whole base load and peak load blocks, and a load following contract whose energy is priced in tiered segments. We decide the plant's operating stage over time, the power taken from each source in every slice, how many base load and peak load contracts to buy, and how the load following energy is split across its price segments. The objective is to make the total cost of meeting the day's demand as small as possible.

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the time slices of the planning day in chronological order, each one a quarter-hour block. The first slice has no predecessor and any slice after the first has the immediately preceding slice as its previous period

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the operating stages the power plant can sit in, listed from lowest to highest. The first stage is the idle stage where the plant produces no power, and each later stage produces more power than the one before it

sets.name: iS
sets.doc: an index used only to define a sliding window of consecutive time slices for the minimum dwell rule on stage changes; its size sets how many consecutive slices the window spans

sets.name: iI
sets.doc: an index used only to define a sliding window of consecutive time slices for the minimum idle rule on plant start ups; its size sets how many consecutive slices the window spans

sets.name: b
sets.doc: the price segments of the load following contract, listed from the first segment upward. Each segment covers a band of daily energy and carries its own per unit energy price, with later segments cheaper than earlier ones

params.name: PowerForecast
params.doc: the forecast electric power that must be served in each time slice, in megawatts

params.name: cPPvar
params.doc: the variable running cost of the power plant, in euro per megawatt hour

params.name: pPPMax
params.doc: the maximal power capacity of the power plant, in megawatts

params.name: cBL
params.doc: the cost of one base load contract, in euro per megawatt hour. A base load contract delivers a constant block of power across every time slice of the day

params.name: cPL
params.doc: the cost of one peak load contract, in euro per megawatt hour. A peak load contract delivers extra power only during the daytime peak slices

params.name: pLFCref
params.doc: the power reference level for the load following contract, in megawatts; it is the per slice upper bound on power drawn from that contract

params.name: eLFCbY
params.doc: the raw yearly daily energy amount marking the upper border of price segment b for the load following contract

params.name: cLFCvar
params.doc: the specific energy price charged within price segment b of the load following contract, in euro per megawatt hour

params.name: eLFCb
params.doc: the daily energy border of price segment b for the load following contract, in megawatt hours per day; it is the cumulative daily energy at the top of segment b

params.name: cLFCs
params.doc: the accumulated fixed cost of the load following contract for all segments filled before segment b, used as the intercept when the chosen segment is b

params.name: IPL
params.doc: a fixed zero or one marker that is one exactly in the daytime peak time slices and zero otherwise; it says when a peak load contract delivers its power

vars.name: c
vars.doc: the total cost of meeting the day's power demand

vars.name: cPP
vars.doc: the total running cost of the power plant over the day

vars.name: pPP
vars.doc: the power drawn from the power plant in each time slice, in megawatts

vars.name: delta
vars.doc: one if the power plant is operating in stage m during time slice t, and zero otherwise

vars.name: chiS
vars.doc: a marker that is forced up to one whenever the plant's operating stage changes going into time slice t

vars.name: chiI
vars.doc: a marker that is forced up to one whenever the plant leaves its idle stage and starts up going into time slice t

vars.name: cSM
vars.doc: the total cost of energy bought through the spot market contracts over the day

vars.name: pSM
vars.doc: the power supplied from the spot market contracts in each time slice, in megawatts

vars.name: alpha
vars.doc: the number of base load contracts purchased; each one delivers a constant block of power across every slice

vars.name: beta
vars.doc: the number of peak load contracts purchased; each one delivers extra power only during the peak slices

vars.name: cLFC
vars.doc: the total cost of the load following contract over the day

vars.name: eLFCtot
vars.doc: the total daily energy drawn through the load following contract, in megawatt hours

vars.name: eLFCs
vars.doc: the daily energy drawn through the load following contract that falls inside price segment b

vars.name: pLFC
vars.doc: the power drawn through the load following contract in each time slice, in megawatts, capped at the reference level

vars.name: mu
vars.doc: one if price segment b is the segment the day's load following energy lands in, and zero otherwise; exactly one segment is chosen

constraints.name: c
constraints.intent: Define the day's total cost as the running cost of the power plant plus the cost of the spot market contracts plus the cost of the load following contract, and set the total cost variable equal to that sum.

constraints.name: pPP[t]
constraints.intent: In every time slice the power served must exactly meet the forecast demand. Add up the power drawn from the power plant, the power supplied by the spot market contracts, and the power taken through the load following contract in that slice, and require the total to equal the forecast power for that slice.

constraints.name: cPP
constraints.intent: Define the power plant's total running cost. Take the plant power drawn in each slice, convert it to energy for that quarter-hour slice, add this up over the whole day, charge it at the plant's variable running rate, and set the plant cost variable equal to that amount.

constraints.name: m)
constraints.intent: In each time slice the power produced by the plant is determined by which operating stage it is in. The idle stage produces nothing, and every higher stage produces a larger share of the plant's maximum capacity, increasing step by step as the stage rises. Set the plant power for the slice equal to the capacity share of whichever stage is active in that slice.

constraints.name: delta[mm,
constraints.intent: In every time slice the power plant must sit in exactly one operating stage. Across all the stages, require that one and only one is active in that slice.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every stage and for every time slice that has a preceding slice, force the stage change marker for that slice up to at least how much that stage's activity rose from the previous slice to the current slice. Do not impose anything for the very first slice, which has no predecessor.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every stage and for every time slice that has a preceding slice, force the stage change marker for that slice up to at least how much that stage's activity fell from the previous slice to the current slice. Together with the companion rule on increases, this makes the marker rise whenever the stage flips either on or off. Do not impose anything for the very first slice, which has no predecessor.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: Limit how often the plant is allowed to change its operating stage so that a stage change must hold for a minimum number of consecutive slices. Looking at each sliding window of consecutive slices of that minimum length across the day, allow at most one stage change to occur inside the window. Apply this only to the windows that fit fully within the planning horizon.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every time slice that has a preceding slice, force the start up marker for that slice up to at least how much the plant left the idle stage going from the previous slice into the current slice. The marker turns on when the plant was idle in the previous slice and is no longer idle in the current slice. Do not impose anything for the very first slice, which has no predecessor.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: Limit how often the plant is allowed to start up so that a start up must be followed by a minimum number of consecutive slices before another is allowed. Looking at each sliding window of consecutive slices of that minimum length across the day, allow at most one start up to occur inside the window. Apply this only to the windows that fit fully within the planning horizon.

constraints.name: cSM
constraints.intent: Define the total cost of the spot market contracts. Each base load contract delivers its block across all the slices of the day and is charged accordingly at the base load rate, and each peak load contract delivers its block only across the peak slices and is charged accordingly at the peak load rate. Set the spot market cost variable equal to the cost of the purchased base load contracts plus the cost of the purchased peak load contracts.

constraints.name: pSM[t]
constraints.intent: In every time slice the power supplied by the spot market is the power from the base load contracts, which deliver in every slice, plus the power from the peak load contracts, which deliver only in the peak slices. Set the spot market power for the slice equal to the base load contracts plus, in the peak slices only, the peak load contracts.

constraints.name: cLFC
constraints.intent: Define the total cost of the load following contract from its tiered pricing. For the chosen price segment there is an accumulated fixed cost for everything filled below it, and on top of that the energy drawn within each segment is charged at that segment's own price. Set the load following cost variable equal to the accumulated fixed cost of the chosen segment plus the per segment energy charged at each segment's price, summed over all segments.

constraints.name: eLFCtot
constraints.intent: Define the total daily energy drawn through the load following contract. Take the load following power in each slice, convert it to energy for that quarter-hour slice, add this up over the whole day, and set the total load following energy variable equal to that amount.

constraints.name: mu[b]
constraints.intent: Exactly one price segment of the load following contract must be selected. Across all the segments, require that one and only one is chosen.

constraints.name: b)
constraints.intent: Tie the total load following energy to the selected price segment and the per segment energy amounts. The total energy must equal the full energy of all the segments filled below the chosen one, taken at their borders, plus the energy drawn within each segment summed over all segments. This forces the lower segments to be completely filled before the chosen segment carries the remainder.

constraints.name: b)[0]
constraints.intent: Cap the energy that can be drawn within the first price segment of the load following contract. The energy assigned to the first segment may not exceed that segment's full band width, and only if the first segment is the chosen one is any energy allowed there at all.

constraints.name: b)
constraints.intent: Cap the energy that can be drawn within each price segment beyond the first of the load following contract. The energy assigned to a segment may not exceed that segment's own band width, which is the gap between its border and the border of the segment just below it, and only if that segment is the chosen one is any energy allowed there at all. Do not impose this on the first segment.

query: Suppose grid rules limit how much we can pull from the spot market in any quarter-hour to 80 MW. What's the resulting total procurement cost under that per-slice cap?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_spot = Constraint(model.t, rule=lambda m, t: m.pSM[t] <= 80)

query: Let's stress-test a tight spot-market limit of 50 MW per quarter-hour, in case liquidity dries up. What does the day cost under that constraint?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_spot = Constraint(model.t, rule=lambda m, t: m.pSM[t] <= 50)

For the chosen price segment there is an accumulated fixed cost for everything filled below it, and on top of that the energy drawn within each segment is charged at that segment's own price. Set the load following cost variable equal to the accumulated fixed cost of the chosen segment plus the per segment energy charged at each segment's price, summed over all segments.

constraints.name: eLFCtot
constraints.intent: Define the total daily energy drawn through the load following contract. Take the load following power in each slice, convert it to energy for that quarter-hour slice, add this up over the whole day, and set the total load following energy variable equal to that amount.

constraints.name: mu[b]
constraints.intent: Exactly one price segment of the load following contract must be selected. Across all the segments, require that one and only one is chosen.

constraints.name: b)
constraints.intent: Tie the total load following energy to the selected price segment and the per segment energy amounts. The total energy must equal the full energy of all the segments filled below the chosen one, taken at their borders, plus the energy drawn within each segment summed over all segments. This forces the lower segments to be completely filled before the chosen segment carries the remainder.

constraints.name: b)[0]
constraints.intent: Cap the energy that can be drawn within the first price segment of the load following contract. The energy assigned to the first segment may not exceed that segment's full band width, and only if the first segment is the chosen one is any energy allowed there at all.

constraints.name: b)
constraints.intent: Cap the energy that can be drawn within each price segment beyond the first of the load following contract. The energy assigned to a segment may not exceed that segment's own band width, which is the gap between its border and the border of the segment just below it, and only if that segment is the chosen one is any energy allowed there at all. Do not impose this on the first segment.

query: Why are we tied into spot-market contracts at all? Surely the on-site plant plus load-following could carry the whole day if we just took zero base and zero peak contracts. What's the model gaining from those contracts?

model_narrative: This is a daily power procurement and unit commitment problem for an electric utility. Over a sequence of quarter-hour time slices we must serve a forecast power demand in every slice. Power can come from three sources: our own power plant, which we run by choosing an operating stage in each slice, spot market contracts that we buy as whole base load and peak load blocks, and a load following contract whose energy is priced in tiered segments. We decide the plant's operating stage over time, the power taken from each source in every slice, how many base load and peak load contracts to buy, and how the load following energy is split across its price segments. The objective is to make the total cost of meeting the day's demand as small as possible.

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the time slices of the planning day in chronological order, each one a quarter-hour block. The first slice has no predecessor and any slice after the first has the immediately preceding slice as its previous period

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the operating stages the power plant can sit in, listed from lowest to highest. The first stage is the idle stage where the plant produces no power, and each later stage produces more power than the one before it

sets.name: iS
sets.doc: an index used only to define a sliding window of consecutive time slices for the minimum dwell rule on stage changes; its size sets how many consecutive slices the window spans

sets.name: iI
sets.doc: an index used only to define a sliding window of consecutive time slices for the minimum idle rule on plant start ups; its size sets how many consecutive slices the window spans

sets.name: b
sets.doc: the price segments of the load following contract, listed from the first segment upward. Each segment covers a band of daily energy and carries its own per unit energy price, with later segments cheaper than earlier ones

params.name: PowerForecast
params.doc: the forecast electric power that must be served in each time slice, in megawatts

params.name: cPPvar
params.doc: the variable running cost of the power plant, in euro per megawatt hour

params.name: pPPMax
params.doc: the maximal power capacity of the power plant, in megawatts

params.name: cBL
params.doc: the cost of one base load contract, in euro per megawatt hour. A base load contract delivers a constant block of power across every time slice of the day

params.name: cPL
params.doc: the cost of one peak load contract, in euro per megawatt hour. A peak load contract delivers extra power only during the daytime peak slices

params.name: pLFCref
params.doc: the power reference level for the load following contract, in megawatts; it is the per slice upper bound on power drawn from that contract

params.name: eLFCbY
params.doc: the raw yearly daily energy amount marking the upper border of price segment b for the load following contract

params.name: cLFCvar
params.doc: the specific energy price charged within price segment b of the load following contract, in euro per megawatt hour

params.name: eLFCb
params.doc: the daily energy border of price segment b for the load following contract, in megawatt hours per day; it is the cumulative daily energy at the top of segment b

params.name: cLFCs
params.doc: the accumulated fixed cost of the load following contract for all segments filled before segment b, used as the intercept when the chosen segment is b

params.name: IPL
params.doc: a fixed zero or one marker that is one exactly in the daytime peak time slices and zero otherwise; it says when a peak load contract delivers its power

vars.name: c
vars.doc: the total cost of meeting the day's power demand

vars.name: cPP
vars.doc: the total running cost of the power plant over the day

vars.name: pPP
vars.doc: the power drawn from the power plant in each time slice, in megawatts

vars.name: delta
vars.doc: one if the power plant is operating in stage m during time slice t, and zero otherwise

vars.name: chiS
vars.doc: a marker that is forced up to one whenever the plant's operating stage changes going into time slice t

vars.name: chiI
vars.doc: a marker that is forced up to one whenever the plant leaves its idle stage and starts up going into time slice t

vars.name: cSM
vars.doc: the total cost of energy bought through the spot market contracts over the day

vars.name: pSM
vars.doc: the power supplied from the spot market contracts in each time slice, in megawatts

vars.name: alpha
vars.doc: the number of base load contracts purchased; each one delivers a constant block of power across every slice

vars.name: beta
vars.doc: the number of peak load contracts purchased; each one delivers extra power only during the peak slices

vars.name: cLFC
vars.doc: the total cost of the load following contract over the day

vars.name: eLFCtot
vars.doc: the total daily energy drawn through the load following contract, in megawatt hours

vars.name: eLFCs
vars.doc: the daily energy drawn through the load following contract that falls inside price segment b

vars.name: pLFC
vars.doc: the power drawn through the load following contract in each time slice, in megawatts, capped at the reference level

vars.name: mu
vars.doc: one if price segment b is the segment the day's load following energy lands in, and zero otherwise; exactly one segment is chosen

constraints.name: c
constraints.intent: Define the day's total cost as the running cost of the power plant plus the cost of the spot market contracts plus the cost of the load following contract, and set the total cost variable equal to that sum.

constraints.name: pPP[t]
constraints.intent: In every time slice the power served must exactly meet the forecast demand. Add up the power drawn from the power plant, the power supplied by the spot market contracts, and the power taken through the load following contract in that slice, and require the total to equal the forecast power for that slice.

constraints.name: cPP
constraints.intent: Define the power plant's total running cost. Take the plant power drawn in each slice, convert it to energy for that quarter-hour slice, add this up over the whole day, charge it at the plant's variable running rate, and set the plant cost variable equal to that amount.

constraints.name: m)
constraints.intent: In each time slice the power produced by the plant is determined by which operating stage it is in. The idle stage produces nothing, and every higher stage produces a larger share of the plant's maximum capacity, increasing step by step as the stage rises. Set the plant power for the slice equal to the capacity share of whichever stage is active in that slice.

constraints.name: delta[mm,
constraints.intent: In every time slice the power plant must sit in exactly one operating stage. Across all the stages, require that one and only one is active in that slice.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every stage and for every time slice that has a preceding slice, force the stage change marker for that slice up to at least how much that stage's activity rose from the previous slice to the current slice. Do not impose anything for the very first slice, which has no predecessor.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every stage and for every time slice that has a preceding slice, force the stage change marker for that slice up to at least how much that stage's activity fell from the previous slice to the current slice. Together with the companion rule on increases, this makes the marker rise whenever the stage flips either on or off. Do not impose anything for the very first slice, which has no predecessor.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: Limit how often the plant is allowed to change its operating stage so that a stage change must hold for a minimum number of consecutive slices. Looking at each sliding window of consecutive slices of that minimum length across the day, allow at most one stage change to occur inside the window. Apply this only to the windows that fit fully within the planning horizon.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: For every time slice that has a preceding slice, force the start up marker for that slice up to at least how much the plant left the idle stage going from the previous slice into the current slice. The marker turns on when the plant was idle in the previous slice and is no longer idle in the current slice. Do not impose anything for the very first slice, which has no predecessor.

constraints.name: t)
constraints.intent: Limit how often the plant is allowed to start up so that a start up must be followed by a minimum number of consecutive slices before another is allowed. Looking at each sliding window of consecutive slices of that minimum length across the day, allow at most one start up to occur inside the window. Apply this only to the windows that fit fully within the planning horizon.

constraints.name: cSM
constraints.intent: Define the total cost of the spot market contracts. Each base load contract delivers its block across all the slices of the day and is charged accordingly at the base load rate, and each peak load contract delivers its block only across the peak slices and is charged accordingly at the peak load rate. Set the spot market cost variable equal to the cost of the purchased base load contracts plus the cost of the purchased peak load contracts.

constraints.name: pSM[t]
constraints.intent: In every time slice the power supplied by the spot market is the power from the base load contracts, which deliver in every slice, plus the power from the peak load contracts, which deliver only in the peak slices. Set the spot market power for the slice equal to the base load contracts plus, in the peak slices only, the peak load contracts.

constraints.name: cLFC
constraints.intent: Define the total cost of the load following contract from its tiered pricing. For the chosen price segment there is an accumulated fixed cost for everything filled below it, and on top of that the energy drawn within each segment is charged at that segment's own price. Set the load following cost variable equal to the accumulated fixed cost of the chosen segment plus the per segment energy charged at each segment's price, summed over all segments.

constraints.name: eLFCtot
constraints.intent: Define the total daily energy drawn through the load following contract. Take the load following power in each slice, convert it to energy for that quarter-hour slice, add this up over the whole day, and set the total load following energy variable equal to that amount.

constraints.name: mu[b]
constraints.intent: Exactly one price segment of the load following contract must be selected. Across all the segments, require that one and only one is chosen.

constraints.name: b)
constraints.intent: Tie the total load following energy to the selected price segment and the per segment energy amounts. The total energy must equal the full energy of all the segments filled below the chosen one, taken at their borders, plus the energy drawn within each segment summed over all segments. This forces the lower segments to be completely filled before the chosen segment carries the remainder.

constraints.name: b)[0]
constraints.intent: Cap the energy that can be drawn within the first price segment of the load following contract. The energy assigned to the first segment may not exceed that segment's full band width, and only if the first segment is the chosen one is any energy allowed there at all.

constraints.name: b)
constraints.intent: Cap the energy that can be drawn within each price segment beyond the first of the load following contract. The energy assigned to a segment may not exceed that segment's own band width, which is the gap between its border and the border of the segment just below it, and only if that segment is the chosen one is any energy allowed there at all. Do not impose this on the first segment.

query: Why bother cycling the plant through stages at all? Just leave it parked in its idle stage all day and cover the whole forecast from contracts and load-following. How much more expensive is that do-nothing-plant plan?
expected_pyomo: model.idle_all = Constraint(model.t, rule=lambda m, t: m.delta['m1', t] == 1)
